name: French Revolution
description: Test your knowledge on the pivotal events, figures, and impacts of the French Revolution.

questions.question: What was the primary cause of the French Revolution?
questions.explain: The French Revolution was primarily fueled by severe economic problems, including famine and national debt, coupled with deep-seated social inequalities where the Third Estate bore most burdens.

questions.question: Which revolutionary leader was a key figure during the Reign of Terror and was eventually guillotined himself?
questions.explain: Maximilien Robespierre was a prominent figure in the Committee of Public Safety and a driving force behind the Reign of Terror, before his own downfall and execution.

questions.question: Which event is traditionally considered the start of the French Revolution?
questions.explain: The Storming of the Bastille on July 14, 1789, symbolized the beginning of the popular uprising against the monarchy and became a powerful symbol of the revolution.

questions.question: What foundational document, adopted in August 1789, proclaimed the rights of citizens and abolished many feudal privileges?
options: The Declaration of the Rights of Man and of the Citizen
questions.explain: The Declaration of the Rights of Man and of the Citizen was a fundamental document of the French Revolution, defining individual and collective rights and serving as a precursor to the first French constitution.

questions.question: Who was the king of France during the outbreak of the French Revolution?
questions.explain: Louis XVI was the last Bourbon king of France, reigning until the monarchy was abolished during the French Revolution.

questions.question: What period of the French Revolution was characterized by extreme violence and mass executions, led primarily by Maximilien Robespierre?
questions.explain: The Reign of Terror, from 1793 to 1794, was a period of intense political repression and executions of perceived enemies of the revolution, orchestrated by the Committee of Public Safety.

questions.question: What was the famous slogan that encapsulated the ideals of the French Revolution?
questions.explain: The slogan 'Liberty, Equality, Fraternity' (Liberté, égalité, fraternité) became the national motto of France and embodied the core principles of the revolution.

questions.question: Which general rose to prominence during the French Revolution and eventually seized power, ending the revolutionary period?
questions.explain: Napoleon Bonaparte was a highly successful military general who orchestrated a coup d'état in 1799, effectively ending the French Revolution and establishing the Consulate.

questions.question: The French Revolution led to the abolition of the monarchy and the establishment of what form of government?
questions.explain: One of the most significant outcomes of the French Revolution was the overthrow of the Bourbon monarchy and the declaration of the First French Republic.

questions.question: What was the primary economic grievance that fueled widespread discontent in France leading up to the Revolution?
options: Excessive taxation on the Third Estate and national debt
The king's lavish spending on foreign wars
Forced conscription of peasants into the army
questions.explain: The heavy tax burden on the common people and the state's severe debt were major sources of anger and resentment.
